bead.title: ddx work: operator-reopening a wedge-parked bead must clear the wedge marker (D3 follow-up; live regression)
bead.description:
PROBLEM (regression found live 2026-06-03; D3 ddx-bd47e2c4 shipped this incomplete)
Operator-reopening a consecutive-wedge-parked bead via `ddx bead update <id> --status open` does NOT clear the work-consecutive-wedges marker, so on the next claim the loop's consecutiveWedgeGuardTrips (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1745) reads the stale count>=threshold and instantly re-parks the bead ("consecutive-wedge guard: <id> wedged 2 consecutive claims; flagged for operator attention"), WITHOUT attempting it. Observed: 7 beads un-parked with --status open all re-wedged at claim until the marker was manually `--unset work-consecutive-wedges`. D3's AC #2 ("a reopened bead is claimable and attempted, not instantly re-parked") is therefore NOT satisfied in production even though the bead closed.

ROOT CAUSE
clearConsecutiveWedge (execute_bead_loop.go ~5844) is only called on a clean guard-free completion (~2604). Nothing clears the marker (or makes consecutiveWedgeGuardTrips return false) when a bead transitions back to an execution-eligible state by OPERATOR action (status proposed/blocked -> open). The reopen and the marker live in different layers (store update vs loop guard state).

PROPOSED FIX
When the loop encounters a ready bead whose status was reopened by an operator after the wedge was recorded (e.g. the wedge marker's timestamp predates the bead's last status->open transition, OR a dedicated reopened-since marker), consecutiveWedgeGuardTrips must return false AND clearConsecutiveWedge must run so the bead gets a fresh attempt. Simplest robust option: clear the work-consecutive-wedges marker at the store layer whenever a bead is transitioned to status=open from proposed/blocked (so any reopen path — CLI or programmatic — drops the stale wedge state). Add the operator-reopen clear so no manual --unset is ever needed.

ACCEPTANCE
1. New test: a bead with work-consecutive-wedges{count>=threshold} transitioned proposed->open (operator reopen) has NO wedge marker afterward (cleared at the store transition), OR consecutiveWedgeGuardTrips returns false for it on the immediately-following claim. Assert the loop ATTEMPTS it (claims + dispatches) rather than re-parking.
2. Regression: simulate the exact observed sequence (record wedge count=2, set status=open, run one drain iteration) and assert the bead is claimed/attempted, not re-flagged operator-attention.
3. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... green.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
